response.justification: Exceptional individual contributor with 17+ years of technical depth and proven expertise in infrastructure, developer tooling, and system design. However, the resume shows zero evidence of people management, direct reports, or team leadership—a hard requirement stating 'minimum 2 years in people management or team leadership.' While teaching ability and senior titles are present, there is no demonstration of performance feedback, team development, or people-focused accountability central to this mid-senior manager role. Recommend as a strong candidate for senior IC or staff engineer positions instead.
strengths: 17+ years software development with multi-platform expertise (web, mobile, embedded, infrastructure)
Hands-on developer tools and infrastructure background (LabWindows IDE at NI, GitLab/CI/CD setup, current MCP tooling work)
Demonstrated ability to teach and build community through workshops, courses, and open-source projects
concerns: No evidence of people management, direct reports, or team leadership—a stated minimum requirement entirely absent from resume
Senior/staff titles lack clarity on scope of technical leadership responsibilities such as architecture decisions or team strategy
Limited focus on core manager competencies: performance expectations, one-on-ones, professional development, and roadmap planning

key_factors.factor: Zero people management experience vs. 2-year minimum requirement
Strong technical depth and infrastructure/tooling expertise
Unclear technical leadership scope in prior roles
